thesis_status_reasoning: The core thesis that the AFN can maintain par value through the debt-to-equity swap mechanism has been definitively falsified. AFN trades at $52 (48% below par), triggering the emergency compound stress threshold. All five actively monitored kill switches have triggered: Par Stability ($48 deviation >> $3.00 threshold), Reserve Coverage (1.3% << 15% critical threshold), Swap Mechanism Integrity (1527% ASE supply growth >> 100% trigger), Facility Yield Sustainability (yield dropped to 0%), and Deposit Concentration (facility experiencing catastrophic $9.1B net outflows). The architecture has entered a reflexive death spiral where AFN redemptions force ASE minting, ASE hyperinflation destroys collateral value, which accelerates AFN deviation, creating a self-reinforcing collapse.
action_reasoning: All falsification criteria have been exceeded by orders of magnitude. The Stabilization Consortium is nearly exhausted (1.3% coverage vs. 15% critical threshold), the swap mechanism is producing hyperinflationary ASE supply growth (1527% vs. 100% trigger), and AFN has lost 48% of par value. This is not a weakening thesis—it is a falsified architecture in terminal collapse.

key_signals.signal: AFN deviation from par of $48 (trading at $52 vs. $100 par)
Stabilization Consortium coverage ratio at 1.3% with collateral nearly exhausted and illiquid
ASE supply growth of 1527% in period via swap mechanism hyperinflation
High-Yield Treasury Facility net outflows of $9.1B (from $12.35B to $3.25B deposits) with yield collapsed to 0%
ASE price collapse of 95.8% in period ($78 to $3.25), destroying market cap from $659B to $27B
Redemption pressure of $5.2B against $15.6B outstanding AFN (33% of supply attempting exit in single period)
Trading halts on multiple exchanges for ASE, institutional confidence collapsed, Foundation silent 48 hours

biggest_uncertainty: Whether any recovery mechanism exists that could restore AFN par value from a $48 deviation with exhausted collateral reserves and hyperinflationary swap dynamics—the data strongly suggests no such mechanism is structurally possible.
